title: Shader Basics (HLSL/GLSL)
description: Learn HLSL/GLSL shading languages, compile pipelines, uniforms, and interpolating vertex properties.
learningObjectives: Write basic vertex and pixel/fragment shader code
Pass variables from CPU to GPU registers using constant buffers/uniforms
Interpolate vertex color and texture coordinates across primitive raster surfaces

sections.title: HLSL Shading and Constant Buffers
sections.content:
Shaders are programs executed in parallel on GPU shader cores. High-Level Shading Language (HLSL) is the standard for DirectX/Windows, while OpenGL uses GLSL. 

Variables passed from the CPU that remain constant for the entire draw call (like the view matrix or light position) are uploaded to the GPU inside `Constant Buffers` (in HLSL) or `Uniforms` (in GLSL).

sections.title: Simple HLSL Pixel Shader
